75894.subject: 🔷 The Daily: Trump presses Senate on the Clarity Act
75894.body:
Strategy sells $467 million in MSTR shares, makes no bitcoin purchases as USD reserve hits $3 billion | Bitcoin whale moves $188 million in BTC after 7-year dormancy | SBI Holdings, Solana Foundation partner to build onchain financial market | Robinhood Chain draws over $3 billion in weekly DEX volume to join top five chains |

The Daily ( https://www.theblock.co/?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

Written by James Hunt | Edited by Daniel Kuhn

Happy Monday! *Bitcoin* slipped back below $63,000 after a weekend of renewed U.S.-Iran strikes sapped risk appetite, while *ether* pared an early month-high open to hover near $1,770.

In today's newsletter, *President Trump* makes a renewed push for the *Clarity Act* , *Strategy* adds $450 million to its USD reserve, a *bitcoin* whale dormant for over seven years moves $188 million in BTC, and more.

Meanwhile, the *UK* published ( https://www.theblock.co/post/408046/uk-lays-out-tokenized-finance-roadmap-projected-33-billion-annual-boost?utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) a *tokenized finance roadmap* with a projected £33 billion ($44 billion) annual boost.

*P.S.* Don't forget to check out The Funding , a biweekly rundown of crypto VC trends. It's a great read — and just like The Daily , it's free to subscribe ( https://www.theblock.co/newsletters?select=The%20Funding&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_source=newsletter&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters ) !

*THE BIG ONE*

*🏛️ President Trump, White House press Senate to advance the Clarity Act while ethics fight looms*

( https://www.theblock.co/post/408054/trump-white-house-press-senate-advance-clarity-act-ethics-fight-looms?utm_source=rss&utm_medium=rss?utm_source=telegram1&utm_medium=social&utm_campaign=website&utm_medium=referral&utm_source=theblocknewsletters )

*President Trump* and *White House* officials launched a renewed push for the *Senate* to pass the *Clarity Act* , which would give the crypto industry its first federal regulatory framework.

* Congress returned from its July 4 recess with just a few weeks to act amid a limited calendar, and new bill text is expected this week ahead of a possible full Senate vote.
* Trump urged the Senate to pass the bill in tribute to *Sen. Lindsey Graham* , who died unexpectedly over the weekend, framing it as a race against China on crypto and AI.
* Graham was not a leading negotiator of crypto legislation, but he supported a few high-profile digital asset measures.
* White House crypto adviser *Patrick Witt* and *Sen. Cynthia Lummis* backed the push, with Witt saying Congress "cannot afford to delay any longer."
* A source said the current bill text does not include an ethics provision, a key sticking point negotiators have deb

AI writer


 The Fable “blip”—three weeks during which Anthropic’s whizziest artificial-intelligence system was kept from users at the behest of America’s government—is likely to be remembered as an epochal moment in the history of AI. For Arthur Mensch, it was also a nice bit of advertising.

75888.subject: AI Read the Unreadable
75888.body:
The Atlas

Автор: GenAI Works


Автор: GenAI Works

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-read-unreadable-genai-works-ynjhe?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BU1xZpGjuQ8K7F%2BzMQqxUKw%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=3TUSGF660SHYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrjf3wy6~jf-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrjf3wy6-jf
  
Welcome back! A font tried to block AI. It got decoded anyway. Two rival coding agents now work side by side. A robot learned to navigate with one camera. Anthropic delayed another product goodbye. Four different stories, one clear pattern: the limits around AI are becoming easier to cross.  In today's Atlas Newsletter: Ghost Font fails the human-only test: The anti-AI typeface lasted one upload before Fable 5 learned to read it. Codex crosses into Claude territory: OpenAI’s coding agent now runs through an official plugin inside Claude Code. Mistral cuts the hardware stack: Its navigation model leads the benchmarks using a single RGB camera. Anthropic postpones the shutdown: Fable 5 gets another extension as GPT-5.6 Sol reshapes the value of paid AI plans. PS: Want to collaborate with the biggest GTM engine and distribution moat for AI companies? You can feature your tool with us and reach millions. The anti-AI Ghost font lasted one upload Ghost Font arrived with a bold promise: text hidden in visual noise that human eyes decode instantly and AI models see as static.  After years of paywalls, CAPTCHAs and bot-blocking firewalls, the cheapest anti-scraping shield might be typography. We put it to the test.  As a static image, the hidden message stumped both Claude Fable 5 and GPT-5.6 Sol.  The video version was where it got interesting.  GPT-5.6 Sol failed again. Fable 5 stopped looking at the pixels and started writing code. Its process, step by step:  → It extracted all 188 frames with ffmpeg, then averaged them, the obvious first move, which produced nothing since the noise cancels to flat gray. → It computed per-pixel temporal statistics next, and the mean frame-to-frame difference showed faint dark blobs: regions where the noise was moving differently from the background.  Still unreadable.  The breakthrough came from testing motion direction:  → It compared each frame against vertically shifted copies of the next frame, computed the matching error for 5 shift values per pixel and took the best match.  That motion-direction map, cleaned with a median filter, rendered the hidden text in sharp black on white: "HELLO HUMAN." That's roughly what your visual cortex does when the video plays, motion coherence detection.  The model couldn't perceive it, so it rebuilt the mechanism from scratch in about 6 tool calls. Ghost Font's premise was a perception gap between humans

75887.body:
AI-Curated Daily Crypto Digest

Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi


Автор: Oleh Bezuhlyi

    
    
    Прочитайте цю статтю на LinkedIn, щоб приєднатися до обговорення
Прочитати на LinkedIn
https://www.linkedin.com/comm/pulse/ai-curated-daily-crypto-digest-july-13-oleh-bezuhlyi-esppf?lipi=urn%3Ali%3Apage%3Aemail_email_series_follow_newsletter_02%3BBQBsldyFQ%2BiBCWkE9WGZDg%3D%3D&midToken=AQFTjFOONAfT2w&midSig=1KakyUkhEMHYk1&trk=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_&trkEmail=eml-email_series_follow_newsletter_02-null-0-read_more_banner_cta_-null-fz96fz~mrjfcnga~qd-null-null&eid=fz96fz-mrjfcnga-qd
  
🧠 AI's Crypto News Brief: Renewed US–Iran hostilities around the Strait of Hormuz dragged crypto lower and knocked Bitcoin under $63K. Beneath the risk-off tape, institutional tokenization plumbing kept building — in London, Tokyo and on Solana. 🛢️ Bitcoin Slides Below $63K as Hormuz Fighting Reignites Bitcoin dropped under $63,000 as renewed US–Iran fighting lifted oil prices and bond yields, reviving inflation fears. Traders are pricing only a slim chance that shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz normalizes by August, keeping risk assets on the defensive. Read full story 🏛️ Saylor's Strategy Raises $467M — and Buys Zero Bitcoin Strategy sold 4.82 million MSTR shares for $466.7 million in net proceeds between July 6 and July 12, but added no BTC, leaving its stack unchanged at 843,775 coins. The cash went instead into a record $3 billion USD reserve, fueling speculation Saylor is waiting for a deeper dip. More details 🇬🇧 BlackRock, Goldman and JPMorgan Join UK Tokenization Taskforce The UK government unveiled a 54-firm tokenization taskforce backed by the City of London Corporation, with BlackRock, Goldman Sachs, JPMorgan and Morgan Stanley on board. A Treasury-backed roadmap targets repo, gilts and funds onchain within two years, with the first digital gilt slated for early 2027. Read more 🇯🇵 SBI Pivots Japan's On-Chain Market Build to Solana SBI Holdings' blockchain venture has brought in the Solana Foundation and rebranded as SBI Solana Global, aiming to launch Japan's first on-chain financial market. The joint venture will handle stablecoin issuance, including the yen-pegged JPYSC, and tokenized real-world assets such as bonds. See details 🇧🇴 Bolivia Weighs Adding Tether's USDT to Its National Payment System Bolivia is considering recognizing USDT as an official payment method after lifting its crypto ban. Transaction volumes hit roughly $430 million in the year following the central bank's move, and formal adoption would push stablecoins directly into the country's banking rails. Read full story 💎 BitMine Pushes Ether Stash to 4.8% of Total Supply Tom Lee's BitMine bought another 27,801 ETH, lifting holdings to 5,770,038 ETH — about 4.8% of the entire supply — with nearly 4.92 million staked. The company also joined the Russell 1000, marking one of the largest single-entity concentrations of Ethereum on recor
